researchQuestions.question: Why do users struggle to discover new music?

topFindings.title: Why users struggle to discover new music
topFindings.summary: Across 146 discovery-related reviews (54 non-discovery excluded): Dominant themes: Other Discovery Frustration (77%), Repetition Fatigue (17%), Positive Discovery Experience (3%). Primary barriers: Unclear Discovery Struggle (95%), Similar Artist Loop (2%), Genre Saturation (1%). Leading root cause: Unclear Repetition Cause (97%).

quotes.text: Not sure what you mean here. a) Discover Weekly according to Spotify is identical to New Music. Every week you get a new playlist that has new releases that they think you will like based on previous
Premium is too expensive but at least Discover Weekly still helps me find new artists worth the cost.
This is great to hear, though I'm curious why photos on your phone / pinterest would be relevant to a recommendation system? Surely the biggest signal would be what Spotify already uses: the features

researchQuestions.question: What listening behaviors are users trying to achieve?

topFindings.title: Use Algorithmic Playlists
topFindings.summary: Users report use algorithmic playlists in 45% of discovery-related reviews.

quotes.text: Stuck in a genre bubble — every Discover Weekly is indie folk even though I listen to hip hop too.
I hear the same artists every Discover Weekly. Zero novelty.
Discover Weekly has introduced me to dozens of artists I now listen to every day. It's one of Spotify's best features.

topFindings.title: Evaluate Recommendations
topFindings.summary: Users report evaluate recommendations in 41% of discovery-related reviews.

quotes.text: Recommendations trapped in one genre despite years of varied listening history.
my favorite music app for over a decade! a happy customer! I love being able to listen to entire albums, make playlists, and most recently I'm super enjoying the AI DJ X, which usually is spot on for

researchQuestions.question: What causes users to repeatedly listen to the same content?

topFindings.title: Unclear Repetition Cause
topFindings.summary: Repetitive listening linked to Unclear Repetition Cause (97% of repetition-related reviews).

quotes.text: Stuck in a genre bubble — every Discover Weekly is indie folk even though I listen to hip hop too.
I hear the same artists every Discover Weekly. Zero novelty.
Discover Weekly has introduced me to dozens of artists I now listen to every day. It's one of Spotify's best features.

topFindings.title: Similarity-Based Reinforcement
topFindings.summary: Repetitive listening linked to Similarity-Based Reinforcement (3% of repetition-related reviews).

topFindings.summary: Other Discovery Frustration affects Unspecified Segment listeners (72%).

pmReadinessScore.rationale: Not ready for PM discovery research — high false-positive rate and fallback dominance.
remainingWeaknesses: LLM false-positive rate 22.2% — classifier keeps non-research reviews.
Unclear Repetition Cause still 96.6% — root-cause insights weak.
Other Discovery Frustration 77.4% — theme bucket still overloaded.
100% of research reviews contain fallback labels.
Few findings include per-field classification_reasons for PM audit.
Limited improvement vs benchmark on key fallback metrics.
